Dummy.notes: Has exactly the same ASM code as Nop.

WaitTime.description: Stop script execution for Time frames, storing the remaining time in the Countdown Variable

ScrCmd_Unused_004.description: Assigns Value to Script Register value

ScrCmd_Unused_005.description: Assigns Value to Script Register value

ScrCmd_Unused_006.description: Assigns data in Address offset to Script Register value

ScrCmd_Unused_007.description: Assigns Value to the Address offset

ScrCmd_Unused_008.description: Assigns Script Register value to the Address offset

ScrCmd_Unused_009.description: Assigns Script Register 2 value to Script Register 1 value

ScrCmd_Unused_00A.description: Assigns data in Address 2 to the Address 1 offset

ScrCmd_Unused_00C.description: Compares Script Register value with Value

ScrCmd_Unused_00D.description: Compares Script Register value with data in Address

ScrCmd_Unused_00E.description: Compares data in Address with Script Register value

ScrCmd_Unused_00F.description: Compares data in Address with Value

ScrCmd_Unused_010.description: Compares data in Address 1 with data in Address 2

CompareVarToVar.description: Compares Variable 1 value with Variable 2 value

ScrCmd_Unused_013.notes: Somehow related with parallel common script execution.

CallCommonScript.notes: See the HGSS sheet for more info. Table in DPPt hasn't been found yet.

ReturnCommonScript.description: Returns to the invoking script/function after a Common Script

GoTo.description: Jumps to a function, Relative Jump bytes forward or backward
GoTo.notes: Relative Jump can have negative values (backward jump).

ScrCmd_Unused_017.description: Jumps to a function, Relative Jump bytes forward or backward, if invoking event has Event ID as ID

ScrCmd_Unused_018.notes: Maybe jumps to a function if invoking event has a specific ID.

ScrCmd_Unused_019.description: Jumps to a function, Relative Jump bytes forward or backward, if player is looking in Player Direction
ScrCmd_Unused_019.notes: Supposed. Needs to be tested in game.

Call.description: Calls a function, Relative Jump bytes forward or backward
Call.notes: Can be used to make loops when calling the own invoking function.

Return.description: Returns to the invoking script/function after a Call

GoToIf.description: If Condition in the condition table, jumps to a function
GoToIf.notes: Ask AdAstra for the condition list. Condition table is updated after a CompareVarValue, CompareVars, CheckFlag...

CallIf.description: If Condition in the condition table, calls a function

CheckFlag.notes: It updates the internal condition flag and maybe part of the condition table.

CheckFlagFromVar.description: Checks if Flag is set and store the result in Variable

SetFlagFromVar.description: Sets the flag whose ID is stored in Flag

SetTrainerFlag.description: Sets the flag of Trainer ID, so marks it as beaten trainer
SetTrainerFlag.notes: So this trainer will not battle against you. Used in every Gym after the leader is beaten.

ClearTrainerFlag.description: Clears the flag of Trainer ID, so marks it as unbeaten trainer
ClearTrainerFlag.notes: So this trainer can be challenged again.

CheckTrainerFlag.description: Checks if Trainer ID has been beaten
CheckTrainerFlag.notes: It updates the internal condition flag and maybe part of the condition table.

AddVar.description: Stores the operation Variable + Operand in Variable

SubVar.description: Stores the operation Variable - Operand in Variable

SetVarFromVar.description: Assigns value of Variable 2 to Variable 1

ScrCmd_Unused_02A.description: Stores Flexible Value (or its value if Variable) in Variable

MessageInstant.description: Display line Text Slot from the text file, all at once

Message.description: Display line Text Slot from the text file

MessageVar.description: Display line Text Slot from the text file
MessageVar.notes: Unlike 002C, this command also accepts a variable as the input field.

WaitABPress.description: Waits for button A or button B to be pressed
WaitABPress.notes: Somehow it uses ScrReg 0 as countdown.

WaitButton.description: This also sets the player's direction appropriately when a pad button is pressed, unlike the WaitABPadPress command.
WaitButton.notes: It seems only A, B, X and direction pad buttons work.

WaitABPadPress.description: As opposed to WaitButton, this does not do a turn frame, i.e. it does not set the player's direction if a pad button is pressed.
WaitABPadPress.notes: Supposed. Needs to be tested in game.

OpenMessage.description: Opens a message box window without printing anything

CloseMessage.notes: SDSME name is misleading, for no button is needed to be pressed.

CloseMessageWithoutErasing.notes: Probably keeps the message box alive after a script. ASM differs with CloseMessage only in a BL instruction.

DrawSignpostInstantMessage.description: Prepares an icon board message box displaying Text Slot message, of Type type, and with Icon image if chosen type supports it
DrawSignpostInstantMessage.notes: Type values from 0 to 3 are: brown (with icon), green (with icon), gray and blue.

DrawSignpostTextBox.description: Prepares a text board message box of Type type, and with Icon image if chosen type supports it
DrawSignpostTextBox.notes: Type values from 0 to 3 are: brown (with icon), green (with icon), gray and blue.

SetSignpostCommand.description: Triggers different Process processes for the board
SetSignpostCommand.notes: Process value 0 keeps the board, value 1 creates the board, value 2 hides the board, value 3 shows the board and value 4 deletes the board.

WaitForSignpostDone.description: Waits the board last process to end

DrawSignpostScrollingMessage.description: Displays Text Slot message in the current text box, stores the current text box status in Variable

GetSignpostInput.description: Returns 1 to Variable if the player has pressed a button so the board must close, 0 otherwise
GetSignpostInput.notes: Variable is stored in ScrReg 0. Command waits for a button to be pressed and stores result in Variable.

ShowYesNoMenu.description: Opens a Yes-No dialog and stores user response in Variable

InitGlobalTextMenu.description: Prepares a multiple selection menu at X and Y screen position, with Cursor default selected option and possibility of cancelling depending on Cancel, with text bank #361, returns selected slot ID in Selection
InitGlobalTextMenu.notes: If Cancel is 1, the user can select the last option by pressing B button.

InitLocalTextMenu.description: Prepares a multiple selection menu at X and Y screen position, with Cursor default selected option and possibility of cancelling depending on Cancel, with local texts, returns selected slot ID in Selection
InitLocalTextMenu.notes: If Cancel is 1, the user can select the last option by pressing B button.

AddMenuEntryImm.description: Adds an option with Multi Slot ID showing Text Slot text

ShowMenu.description: Shows the prepared multi in screen
ShowMenu.notes: The game will hide it when the player has selected an option.

InitGlobalTextListMenu.description: Prepares a multiple selection list at X and Y screen position, with Cursor default selected option and possibility of cancelling depending on Cancel, with text bank #361, returns selected slot ID in Selection
InitGlobalTextListMenu.notes: If Cancel is 1, the user can select the last option by pressing B button. The difference between a Multi and a List seems to be that lists are scrollable.

InitLocalTextListMenu.description: Prepares a multiple selection list at X and Y screen position, with Cursor default selected option and possibility of cancelling depending on Cancel, with local texts, returns selected slot ID in Selection
InitLocalTextListMenu.notes: If Cancel is 1, the user can select the last option by pressing B button. The difference between a Multi and a List seems to be that lists are scrollable.

AddListMenuEntry.description: Adds a new option to the list, with List Slot ID and showing text line Option MSG. It also displays text line Highlight MSG when that option is highlighted.
AddListMenuEntry.notes:
If Highlight MSG is set to 255 [0xFF], no message will be displayed when the option is highlighted.
This command relies on an open message box in order to display the highlight messages. 

Trying to highlight an option with Highlight MSG different than 255 [0xFF] will result in a crash if no message box is already being displayed.

ShowListMenu.description: Shows the prepared list in screen
ShowListMenu.notes: The game will hide it when the player has selected an option.

ShowMenuMultiColumn.description: Splits the multi window in Columns different columns

PlaySE.description: Plays a sound effect without interrupting the current music

WaitCry.description: Waits the current cry to finish

PlayFanfare.description: Pauses the current music and then plays a sound effect

WaitFanfare.description: Waits for Sound to finish, then resumes music

StopMusic.notes: Parameter is ignored because the game identifies what music is playing.

PlayDefaultMusic.description: Plays the default music track based on the player's current location

FadeOutBGM.description: Fades out the current music to Volume in Time frames

FadeInBGM.description: Fades in the current music to normal volume in Time frames

SetBGMPlayerPaused.description: If Status = 1, the track that's currently played by chosen Player ID will pause. If Status = 0, Player ID will resume the previously paused music.
SetBGMPlayerPaused.notes: The track won't restart from the beginning, but from the exact point where it was paused.

SetBGM.description: While in the field overworld, plays Music using SSEQ Player #7 (PLAYER_BGM)

SetBGMFixed.notes: Seems to set a flag related to the music system.

CheckRecordedChatotCryIsPlayable.description: Checks if Chatot has custom sound, stores answer in Variable
CheckRecordedChatotCryIsPlayable.notes: Returns 0 or 1 to the specified Variable. Not for a specific Chatot but for all (they share a custom sound).

TryRecordChatotCry.description: Starts Chatot microphone record, stores answer in Variable
TryRecordChatotCry.notes: Returns 0 or 1 to the specified Variable depending on successful recording.

StoreRecordedChatotCry.notes: Maybe saves the custom Chatot sound in the savegame.

SetSubScene63.description: Loads Spear Pillar cutscene sound data (GROUP_SE_CLIMAX in the SDAT)

ApplyMovement.description: Applies movement at Relative Jump to Event ID
ApplyMovement.notes: It seems that movements must be halfword-aligned in the script file.

WaitMovement.description: Waits for all movements to finish

LockAll.description: Locks every event in the map

ReleaseAll.description: Releases every event in the map

AddObject.description: Add Event ID overworld in the map
AddObject.notes: It is probable that, if executing this having Event ID overworld already in the map, it gets duplicated.

RemoveObject.description: Removes Event ID overworld from the map

AddFreeCamera.description: Locks the camera at position X and Y
AddFreeCamera.notes: Seems that X and Y must be the current player position

FacePlayer.description: Makes the invoking event to look at the player
FacePlayer.notes: In fact it simply takes the current player direction and makes the NPC look at the opposite direction.

GetPlayerMapPos.description: Saves the current player position in X and Y

ScrCmd_Unused_06A.description: Saves the current Event ID position in X and Y

MoveCamera.notes: It has to do with camera movements

SetObjectFlagIsPersistent.description: If Value is 1, prevents Event ID from disappearing when entering a new map header

SetMovementType.description: Changes the Event ID's movement number to Movement

ScrCmd_Unused_06E.description: Assigns ID 0xFE for any overworld that follows the player

GiveMoney.description: Adds Money to the player's money

RemoveMoney.description: Subtracts Money from the player's money

CheckMoney.description: Returns 1 to Variable if the player has Money or more money, returns 0 otherwise

ShowMoney.description: Shows the money box at X and Y coordinates of the screen
ShowMoney.notes: Each X and Y unit implies 8 pixels. Values of 0 or higher than the screen may glitch the screen.

UpdateMoneyDisplay.description: Updates the current money in the money box

ShowCoins.description: Shows the coin box at X and Y coordinates of the screen
ShowCoins.notes: Each X and Y unit implies 8 pixels. Values of 0 or higher than the screen may glitch the screen.

UpdateCoinDisplay.description: Updates the current coins in the coin box

GetCoinsAmount.description: Stores in Variable the current amount of coins

AddCoins.description: Adds Coins to the player's coin case

SubtractCoinsFromValue.description: Subtracts Coins from the player's coin case

AddItem.description: If the player has no space left in the bag, destVarID will be set to FALSE

RemoveItem.description: destVarID will be set to TRUE if the player had at least 'count' items of the specified type in their bag

CanFitItem.description: destVarID will be set to TRUE if the player has space for at least 'count' items of the specified type in their bag

CheckItem.description: destVarID will be set to TRUE if the player has at least 'count' items of the specified type in their bag
CheckItem.notes: Even if the Amount to check for is 0, the check will return 0 if you don't have the item at all.

IsItemTMHM.description: Stores 1 in Variable if Item is TM or HM, stores 0 otherwise

GetItemPocket.description: Stores the bag pocket of Item in Variable

ScrCmd_Dummy081.notes: Script command ASM code seems to be empty.

ScrCmd_Dummy082.notes: Script command ASM code seems to be empty.

SendGoodToPC.description: Adds one Goods item to the underground PC, stores 1 in Variable if it could be stored and stores 0 otherwise

Dummy084.notes: Even though it does nothing, Variable must be a variable.

CheckHasRoomForGoodsInPC.description: Stores 1 in Variable if there's at least one free slot in the underground PC for a goods, stores 0 otherwise

Dummy086.notes: Even though it does nothing, Variable must be a variable.

GiveTrap.description: Adds one Trap item to the underground bag, stores 1 in Variable if it could be stored and stores 0 otherwise

Dummy088.notes: Even though it does nothing, Variable must be a variable.

Dummy089.notes: Even though it does nothing, Variable must be a variable.

Dummy08A.notes: Even though it does nothing, Variable must be a variable.

Dummy08B.notes: Even though it does nothing, Variable must be a variable.

Dummy08C.notes: Even though it does nothing, Variable must be a variable.

Dummy08D.notes: Even though it does nothing, Variable must be a variable.

Dummy08E.notes: Even though it does nothing, Variable must be a variable.

GiveSphere.description: Adds one Sphere item of size Level to the underground bag, stores 1 in Variable if it could be given, stores 0 otherwise

Dummy090.notes: Even though it does nothing, Variable must be a variable.

Dummy091.notes: Even though it does nothing, Variable must be a variable.

Dummy092.notes: Even though it does nothing, Variable must be a variable.

CountSealOccurence.description: Stores in Variable the current amount of Seal ID seals

GiveOrTakeSeal.description: Adds Amount of Seal ID seals if there's enough space for them

GetPartyMonForm.description: Stores in Variable the form of the Pokémon in Party Position
GetPartyMonForm.notes: Pokémon in Party Position doesn't have to be a Unown, it can be any Pokémon.

GivePokemon.description: Gives Pokémon at level Level with an Item, store 1 in Variable if succeed and 0 otherwise

GiveEgg.description: Gives a Pokémon egg received from Location text slot at text bank #435

ResetPartyMonMoveSlot_Unused.description: Replaces Party Position Pokémon move at Move Position move slot by Move ID new move

CheckPartyMonHasMove.description: Stores 1 in Variable if Pokémon at Party Position knows Move ID move, stores 0 otherwise

FindPartySlotWithMove.description: Checks if any Pokémon in the party knows Move ID move, stores in Variable the position of the first Pokémon that knows it or stores 6 if no Pokémon in the party knows the move

ScrCmd_0A6.description: Shows Dress-Up screen with Pokémon at specified Party Pos

ShowDressUpPhoto.description: Stores 0 in Variable if the chosen Portrait Slot was correctly loaded, stores 1 otherwise

ScrCmd_0A8.description: Stores 0 in Variable if the chosen Contest Portrait Slot was correctly loaded, stores 1 otherwise

OpenPokemonStorage.description: Opens a PC screen to perform the chosen Operation.
OpenPokemonStorage.notes:
Operation codes: 
0 -> "Deposit Pokemon"
1 -> "Withdraw Pokemon"
2 -> "Move Pokemon"
3 -> "Move Items"
4 -> "Compare Pokemon"

ClearGame.description: Opens the Hall of Fame and game ending screens

StartChooseStarterScene.description: Opens the starter Pokémon selection screen

SaveChosenStarter.description: Stores the selected starter Pokémon in the save data and frees the Pokémon selection screen resources

ScrCmd_Unused_0BA.description: Opens the keyboard for naming the player, stores 1 in Variable if user cancels the proccess

OpenPokemonNamingScreen.description: Opens the keyboard for naming a Pokémon at Party Position, stores 1 in Variable if user cancels the proccess

FadeScreen.description: Fade screen of Type type with Transition different screens of Time frames each one, to or from Color color
FadeScreen.notes: Type 0 for fade in, Type 1 for fade out. Color has 15-bit format. Transition is the number of color changes that the game does in the fading.

WaitFadeScreen.description: Wait for the screen fade to end

Warp.description: Warps to X and Y position of Map header with initial Dir direction, using Door warp
Warp.notes: When Door is 0 it doesn't use any door animation.

UseRockClimb.description: Uses rock climb with the Pokémon at Party Position position

UseSurf.description: Uses surf with the Pokémon at Party Position position

UseWaterfall.description: Uses waterfall with the Pokémon at Party Position position

UseFly.description: Flies to Map header map, to coordinates X and Y

ScrCmd_0C3.description: Sets weather to 0 (sunny) and then updates the weather graphics

ScrCmd_0C4.description: Sets weather to 0 (sunny) and then updates the weather graphics

ScrCmd_0C5.description: Uses cut with the Pokémon at Party Position position

ChangeIntoContestAttire.description: Changes the current player form to the contest dress

CheckPlayerOnBike.description: Stores 1 in Variable if player is riding the bike, stores 0 otherwise

SetPlayerBike.description: If rideBike == TRUE, sets the player on the bike. If rideBike == FALSE, removes the player from the bike.

ForceBicycling.description: If Action is 1, the game sets the cycling road flag and moves the player down. Otherwise the flag is deactivated.

GetPlayerState.description: Stores in Variable the current player form
GetPlayerState.notes: Values 0 is for normal form, value 1 for riding the bike and value 2 for surfing.

SetPlayerState.description: Activates the Form bit in the internal player form data
SetPlayerState.notes: The purpose of the different bits still needs to be researched.

ChangePlayerState.description: Updates changes in the internal player form data in the screen

BufferPlayerName.description: Stores player's name in String Buffer

BufferRivalName.description: Stores rival's name in String Buffer

BufferCounterpartName.description: Stores counterpart's name in String Buffer

BufferPartyMonSpecies.description: Stores the name of the Pokémon in Party Position in String Buffer

BufferItemName.description: Stores Item's name in String Buffer

BufferPocketName.description: Stores Pocket's name in String Buffer

BufferTMHMMoveName.description: Stores the name of the move contained in Item TM/HM in String Buffer

BufferMoveName.description: Stores battle Move's name in String Buffer

BufferNumber.description: Stores a Number in String Buffer

BufferPartyMonNickname.description: Stores the nickname of the Pokémon in Party Position in String Buffer

BufferPoketchAppName.description: Stores Pokétch App's name in String Buffer

BufferTrainerClassName.description: Stores Trainer's name in String Buffer

BufferTrainerClassFromAppearance.description: Stores player's Union Room trainer type in String Buffer

BufferSpeciesNameFromVar.description: Stores Pokémon's name in String Buffer
BufferSpeciesNameFromVar.notes: The two Unused parameters are for defining male/female nouns and singular/plural nouns respectively. However both don't apply in this command.

BufferPlayerStarterSpeciesName.description: Stores the name of the player' starter Pokémon in String Buffer

BufferRivalStarterSpeciesName.description: Stores the name of the rival' starter Pokémon in String Buffer

BufferPlayerCounterpartStarterSpeciesNam.description: Stores the name of the counterpart' starter Pokémon in String Buffer

GetPlayerStarterSpecies.description: Stores the starter Pokémon ID in Variable

BufferUndergroundGoodsName.description: Stores underground Goods' name in String Buffer

BufferUndergroundTrapName.description: Stores underground Trap's name in String Buffer

BufferUndergroundItemName.description: Stores underground Treasure's name in String Buffer

BufferMapName.description: Stores Map header name in String Buffer

GetSwarmMapAndSpecies.description: Selects a zone depending on a random seed, then stores the zone ID in Map and the corresponding swarm Pokémon of that zone in Pokémon

GetTrainerID.description: Stores the trainer ID number in Variable depending on the script ID
GetTrainerID.notes: This means you shouldn't use this command in a normal script.

StartTrainerBattle.description: Starts a battle against Trainer 1 and, if Trainer 2 is neither 0 nor the same as Trainer 1, a double battle against both

PrintTrainerDialogue.description: Displays the text of Trainer ID trainer at condition Type
PrintTrainerDialogue.notes: The possible Type values are the following (expand the note for see them)

GetTrainerMessageTypes.description: If the script ID corresponds to a single battle trainer, stores 0 in Noticing, 2 in Defeat and 0 in One Pokémon. If it corresponds to a double battle, stores 3, 5 and 6 or 7, 9 and 10 depending on whether it's the first or the second trainer
GetTrainerMessageTypes.notes: This means you shouldn't use this command in a normal script.

GetTrainerRematchMessageTypes.description: If the script ID corresponds to a single battle trainer, stores 17 in Noticing, 0 in Defeat and 0 in One Pokémon. If it corresponds to a double battle, stores 18, 0 and 6 or 19, 0 and 10 depending on whether it's the first or the second trainer
GetTrainerRematchMessageTypes.notes: This means you shouldn't use this command in a normal script.

CheckIsTrainerDoubleBattle.description: Stores 0 in Variable if the script ID corresponds to a single battle, stores 1 if it corresponds to a double battle
CheckIsTrainerDoubleBattle.notes: This means you shouldn't use this command in a normal script.

PlayTrainerEncounterBGM.description: Retrieves the Trainer Class ID of the passed Trainer and plays its eye contact music.

BlackOutFromBattle.description: Return to Pokémon Center or player's house after being defeated

CheckWonBattle.description: Stores 0 in Variable if player has been defeated in battle, stores 1 otherwise

CheckLostBattle.description: Stores 0 in Variable if player defeated or caught the wild Pokemon, stores 1 otherwise

CheckHasTwoAliveMons.description: Stores 1 in Variable if player has at least 2 not fainted Pokémon, stores 0 otherwise

StartDummyTrainerBattle.description: Starts a battle against trainer with ID 1

SetTargetTrainerDefeated.description: Sets the trainer flag with the same ID as the invoking event

GoToIfTargetTrainerDefeated.description: Jumps to a function, Relative Jump bytes forward or backward, if trainer flag with the invoking event ID is set

CheckPartyPokerus.description: Stores 1 in Variable if one or more Pokémon in the party are infected with the Pokérus

GetPartyMonGender_Unused.description: Stores 0 in Variable if Pokémon at Party Position is male, 1 if is female and 2 if gender is unknown
GetPartyMonGender_Unused.notes: Unknown gender value is supposed and should be checked.

SetSpecialLocation.description: Changes the data of the special warp in the current map with Map destination header, X and Y coordinates and Dir player direction, saving Warp in the save data as the last used warp
SetSpecialLocation.notes: Not sure if it sets data for a warp event, or it updates location data in the savegame.

GetFloorsAbove.description: Stores in Variable the current floor in a elevator
GetFloorsAbove.notes: It does something to the value based on the current Header.

ShowCurrentFloor.description: destVar is used to initialize a FieldMenuManager, setting it to 0xEEEE, but is otherwise left without a meaningful value

GetLocalDexSeenCount.description: Stores in Variable the total seen Pokemon in the Sinnoh Pokédex

GetLocalDexCaughtCount_Unused.description: Stores in Variable the total obtained Pokemon in the Sinnoh Pokédex

GetNationalDexSeenCount.description: Stores in Variable the total seen Pokemon in the National Pokédex

GetNationalDexCaughtCount.description: Stores in Variable the total obtained Pokemon in the National Pokédex

LoadPokedexRating.description: Checks the number of seen Pokémon in the Sinnoh Pokédex (if Mode is 0) or the number of obtained Pokémon in the National Pokédex (otherwise) and stores the corresponding Professor message in Variable

StartWildBattle.description: Starts a wild battle against a Pokémon of level Level

StartFirstBattle.description: Starts a first battle against Trainer ID
StartFirstBattle.notes: The difference between this and the TrainerBattle command is that Bag, Run and Pokémon buttons are disabled.

SlatherHoneyTree.description: Updates the internal honey tree data for setting up a new wild encounter

GetHoneyTreeStatus.description: Stores 1 in Variable if player can spread honey in the tree, stores 2 if player already spread honey and stores 3 if the wild encounter is ready

StartHoneyTreeBattle.description: Starts the honey tree wild encounter

StartSignatureApp.notes: Make sure to fade the screen from black after calling this command.

CheckSaveType.description: Stores 0 in Variable if a different savegame exists and it's not possible to save, stores 1 if no previous savegame exists, stores 2 if game has to save a lot of data and stores 3 if game can perform a quick save

TrySaveGame.description: Saves the game and stores 1 in Variable if savegame was succesfully made, stores 0 otherwise

DressUpPhotoHasData.description: Stores 0 in Variable if the chosen Portrait Slot is empty, stores 1 otherwise

ContestPhotoHasData.description: Stores 0 in Variable if the chosen Contest Portrait Slot is empty, stores 1 otherwise

ScrCmd_131.notes: This includes both the touch screen animation and an internal save data update.

CheckPoketchEnabled.description: Stores 1 in Variable if player has the Poketch, stores 0 otherwise

RegisterPoketchApp.description: Adds App Poketch application to the player's Poketch

CheckPoketchAppRegistered.description: Stores 1 in Variable if player has App Poketch application, stores 0 otherwise

PokeMartCommon.description: Opens the common mart shopping screen

PokeMartSpecialties.description: Opens the Mart ID mart shopping screen
PokeMartSpecialties.notes: Used in the Departament Store for a shop with specific fixed items.

PokeMartDecor.description: Opens the Mart ID goods mart shopping screen

PokeMartSeal.description: Opens the Mart ID seal mart shopping screen

BlackOutFromBattle2.description: Return to Pokémon Center or player's house after being defeated

SetBlackOutWarpId.description: Sets map's Warp ID warp as the last used warp
SetBlackOutWarpId.notes: Supposed. This would affect actions like Escape Rope use.

GetPlayerGender.description: Stores 0 in Variable if player is male, stores 1 if female

HealParty.description: Heals every Pokémon in the player's party
HealParty.notes: That does not include the black fade nor the healing sound.

SetCommPlayerDir.description: Turns player towards the chosen Direction (used during communications with other players)

CheckPokedexAcquired.description: Stores 1 in Variable if player has the Pokédex, stores 0 otherwise

GivePokedex.description: Gives the Pokédex to the player

CheckRunningShoesAcquired.description: Stores 1 in Variable if player has the running shoes, stores 0 otherwise

GiveRunningShoes.description: Gives the running shoes to the player

CheckBadgeAcquired.description: Checks if player has Badge ID badge, stores boolean result in Variable

GiveBadge.description: Gives the player Badge ID badge

CountBadgesAcquired.description: Stores in Variable the number of badges obtained

CheckBagAcquired.description: Stores 1 in Variable if player has the bag, stores 0 otherwise

GiveBag.description: Gives the bag to the player

CheckHasPartner.description: Stores 1 in Variable if the following event flag is activated, stores 0 otherwise

SetHasPartner.description: Activates the following event flag, so you can no longer use the bike, surf, rock climb...

ClearHasPartner.description: Deactivates the following event flag, so you can use the bike, surf, rock climb... again

CheckStepFlag.description: Checks if player has moved one step, stores the result in Variable

CheckGameCompleted.description: Stores 1 in Variable if the game has been completed, stores 0 otherwise

LoadDoorAnimation.description: Prepares a building located at Map X and Map Y coordinates in a map located at Matrix X and Matrix Y in the current matrix for animation, with Door ID animation ID

WaitForAnimation.description: Wait animation of building with Door ID animation ID

UnloadAnimation.description: Free animation of building with Door ID animation ID

PlayDoorOpenAnimation.description: Triggers first animation of building with Door ID animation ID

PlayDoorCloseAnimation.description: Triggers second animation of building with Door ID animation ID

BufferDaycareMonNicknames.description: Stores first daycare Pokémon nickname in string buffer 0, the second daycare Pokémon nickname in string buffer 1 and the first Pokémon trainer's name in buffer 2
BufferDaycareMonNicknames.notes: If there's only one Pokémon in the daycare, string buffer 1 does not change. If no Pokémon is in the daycare, commands does nothing.

GetDaycareState.description: If daycare Pokémon have an egg, stores 1 in Variable. Otherwise it stores 0 if no Pokémon is in the daycare, 2 if only one Pokémon is in the daycare or 3 if there are two Pokémon

InitPersistedMapFeaturesForPastoriaGym.description: Initializes Pastoria Gym internal data and 3D model animations

PressPastoriaGymButton.description: Checks if a button 3D model is at player position and moves the water level
PressPastoriaGymButton.notes: The game checks for buildings 239, 240 and 241 in the current player coordinates.

InitPersistedMapFeaturesForHearthomeGym.description: Initializes Hearthome Gym internal data and 3D model animations

MoveHearthomeGymDPLift.description: Triggers a elevator to move up or down
MoveHearthomeGymDPLift.notes: Checks the current player height position to determine the direction.

InitPersistedMapFeaturesForCanalaveGym.description: Initializes Canalave Gym internal data and 3D model animations

InitPersistedMapFeaturesForVeilstoneGym.description: Initializes Veilstone Gym internal data and 3D model animations

InitPersistedMapFeaturesForSunyshoreGym.description: Initializes Sunyshore Gym Room map internal data and 3D model animations

PressSunyshoreGymButton.description: Rotates Sunyshore Gym gears +90º if Rotation is 0, -90º if Rotation is 1 or +180º if Rotation is 2

GetPartyCount.description: Stores in Variable the current number of Pokémon in party

OpenBag.description: Opens the bag with only Normal pocket avalible if Mode is 0, otherwise only with the Berries pocket avalible

GetSelectedItem.description: Stores in Variable the selected item after a BagScreen command, or zero if user cancels the selection

CheckPocketHasItems.description: Stores 1 in Variable if bag's Pocket has at least one item there, stores 0 otherwise

BufferBerryName.description: Stores the name of Item berry in String Buffer, returning a plural noun if Number is greater than 1
BufferBerryName.notes: Item value must be greater or equal than 0x95 (Cheri Berry).

BufferNatureName.description: Stores the name of Nature in String Buffer

GetBerryGrowthStage.description: Stores the current berry growth phase of the invoking event in Variable
GetBerryGrowthStage.notes:
0 = None
1 = Planted
2 = Sprouted
3 = Growing
4 = Blooming
5 = Fruit

GetBerryMulchType.description: Stores the mulch item ID corresponding on the current invoking event berry tree in Variable

GetJubilifeLotteryTrainerID.description: Stores Lotto random number in Variable

CheckForJubilifeLotteryWinner.description:
Takes a Winning Number as input and responds as follows.
If no matching Pokémon could be found or an error occurs: 
MatchPos: 0; Digits: 0; PC: 0.

For all other cases:

If the matching Pokémon is in the Player's party, PC will be assigned 0.
Otherwise, if it's in the Player's Storage System, PC will be assigned 1.

MatchPos stores the zero-based position of the winning Pokémon (either as Party pos or Storage System pos, depending on the value of PC).

When there's a match, the Digits var is assigned the number of matching digits of the Winning Pokémon's PID.
In case of multiple matches, priority is always given to the one with the most matching digits.
CheckForJubilifeLotteryWinner.notes:
To retrieve the exact position of a winning Pokémon in the Storage System, first make sure PC equals 1. 

The zero-based PC Page and Position indices can be calculated:

PC Page index = MatchPos / 30
Position in page = MatchPos % 30

where % is the modulo operator.

Delay1.description: Event waits for 1 frame; Equivalent to ~0.033 seconds (1/30s).

Delay2.description: Event waits for 2 frames; Equivalent to ~0.067 seconds (2/30s).

Delay4.description: Event waits for 4 frames; Equivalent to ~0.133 seconds (4/30s).

Delay8.description: Event waits for 8 frames; Equivalent to ~0.267 seconds (8/30s).

Delay15.description: Event waits for 15 frames; Equivalent to 0.5 seconds (15/30s).

Delay16.description: Event waits for 16 frames; Equivalent to ~0.533 seconds (16/30s).

Delay32.description: Event waits for 32 frames; Equivalent to ~1.067 seconds (32/30s).

WarpOut.description: Makes overworld ascend to the sky. No rotation involved.

WarpIn.description: Makes overworld fall from the sky. No rotation involved.

SetInvisible.description: Makes event invisible. It can still be interacted with and will still have collision.

LockDir.description: Prevents event from changing direction. It can still move, but it will always face the same direction.

UnlockDir.description: Allows event to change direction again.

PauseAnimation.description: Disables event's animation. It can still move, but it will not animate.

EmoteExclamationMark.description: Shows an exclamation mark above the event's head. Used commonly to indicate that the event is surprised or has noticed something.

WalkSlightlyFastNorth.description: Event faces up and walks northwards at 1 tile per 6 frames; Equivalent to 5 tiles per second.

WalkSlightlyFastSouth.description: Event faces down and walks southwards at 1 tile per 6 frames; Equivalent to 5 tiles per second.

WalkSlightlyFastWest.description: Event faces right and walks westwards at 1 tile per 6 frames; Equivalent to 5 tiles per second.

WalkSlightlyFastEast.description: Event faces right and walks eastwards at 1 tile per 6 frames; Equivalent to 5 tiles per second.

WalkSlightlyFasterEast.description: Event faces right and walks eastwards at 1 tile per 3 frames; Equivalent to 10 tiles per second.

WalkFastestNorth.description: Event faces up and walks nothwards at 1 tile per frame; Equivalent to 30 tiles per second. Walk animation isn't triggered.

EmoteDoubleExclamationMark.description: Shows a double exclamation mark above the event's head.

MoveAction_153.description: Shows an exclamation mark above the event's head. Duplicate of EmoteExclamation (0x004B).

EndMovement.description: Action end marker. Used to indicate the end of a movement sequence.
